steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The blank joins two independent clauses: 'The book wasn't written solely by Kittredge' and 'other authors, such as Linda Hogan and Barry Lopez, also contributed essays,' with 'however' marking the contrast. Two valid placements exist for the conjunctive adverb: ending IC1 as a trailing parenthetical (', however;') or starting IC2 ('; however,'). Test which placement the sentence prefers.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Kittredge, however;': The comma binds 'however' to IC1 as a trailing parenthetical, qualifying the claim that the book was written solely by Kittredge. The semicolon then joins IC2 about the other contributors. Parse: [IC1 + trailing 'however'] ; [IC2]. The contrast attaches directly to the assertion about sole authorship — the most natural semantic fit. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Kittredge; however,': Grammatically valid, but placing 'however' at the start of IC2 shifts the emphasis: it frames the list of other authors as the surprising new claim rather than qualifying the sole-authorship assertion. Less idiomatic for this sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Kittredge. However,': Splits one continuous contrast into two sentences, misrepresenting the passage's unified structure. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Kittredge, however,': Comma before and comma after 'however' joins two independent clauses with only commas — a comma splice. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
